phase_name: Proposal Workflow Polish — Client Context, Operations Permissions, Revision Cycle, Audit Trail Integration

completed_tasks.name: Verify plans — iter 2 passed after 2 targeted revisions (W1 enumerated 6 client_name sites; W2 corrected Request Revision grep count)
Decision coverage 26/26 D-NN verified; REQ coverage N/A (Requirements: TBD on phase)

remaining_tasks.name: Wave 1 (parallel) — Execute 87.2-01 (Target Client default + 6-site company_name bug fix) + 87.2-02 (firestore.rules new proposals update branch)
MANUAL DEPLOY — `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules` (REQUIRED before Wave 2's smoke-test or operations_user assignment-check will fail)
Wave 2 — Execute 87.2-03 (renderProposalActionButtons dual canApprove + canDrive flags, parent_collection-aware department gating, _parentDocCache preload)
Wave 3 — Execute 87.2-04 (Request Revision button + sub-modal + extended Mark Sent gate + REVISION_REQUESTED audit action)
Wave 4 — Execute 87.2-05 (renderAuditTrail merges comms_log as nested children under SENT_TO_CLIENT / CREATED)

UAT — manual smoke-test on PROP-2026-001 walkthrough: verify Target Client populates, operations_user sees new buttons, Request Revision flow, comms_log saves succeed for assigned operations_user, audit trail shows nesting
/gsd-verify-phase 87.2 — goal-backward verification against the 5 Roadmap Success Criteria (re-interpreted per D-10/D-11/D-12/D-15 corrective for SC#3 and D-03 corrective for SC#1)

human_actions_pending.context: Plan 87.2-02 modifies firestore.rules — required deploy step between Wave 1 and Wave 2 so the new field-mask + parent-assignment + department-alignment update branch is live before operations_user/services_user smoke-tests. Netlify auto-deploys SPA assets only; Firebase rules are out-of-band.

Five integration gaps were originally surfaced in a super_admin walkthrough of this specific proposal. The same walkthrough is the natural smoke-test post-execution: verify Target Client populates correctly on new proposals from a project context, operations_user sees Mark Sent / Client Approved / Mark as Loss / Request Revision buttons, Request Revision moves status to For Revision, comms_log saves succeed without `Missing or insufficient permissions`, audit trail renders comms as indented children.

decisions.decision: CONTEXT.md mid-discussion course-correction supersedes ROADMAP.md Success Criterion #3
decisions.rationale: ROADMAP says 'Logging a Revision Requested comms entry advances status' (auto-status-from-comms). CONTEXT D-10/D-11/D-12/D-15 explicitly rejects this and replaces it with explicit Request Revision action button + extended Mark Sent gate. Plans MUST honor CONTEXT, not ROADMAP, for SC#3. Comms entries stay pure logs.

decisions.decision: No legacy backfill for target_client_name — D-03 overrides Roadmap SC#1
decisions.rationale: ROADMAP says 'legacy proposals are backfilled on-read from parent'. CONTEXT D-03 explicitly rejects backfill ('just in prod as of now so no need for legacy'). Existing prod proposals with target_client_name=null stay until manually corrected via Edit modal once the save-side bug is fixed. No read-time fallback, no migration script.

decisions.decision: Bundle ALL six client_name sites in Plan 01 (1 write + 3 dropdown render + 2 sort comparator)
decisions.rationale: D-02 originally identified the saveProposal write site only. Plan-checker iter 1 found 3 additional sites in showCreateModal's dropdown render (lines 529-531). Plan-checker iter 2 found 2 more in the _modalClientsData.sort() comparator at line 160 (`a.client_name`, `b.client_name`). Same root cause (canonical field is company_name, not client_name); bundling them keeps the bug fix atomic and avoids a visible 'empty client name' defect during the Plan 01 smoke-test.

decisions.decision: Wave structure prioritizes shared-file safety over speed
decisions.rationale: Plans 01, 03, 04, 05 all touch app/proposal-modal.js. Per project memory `feedback_orphan_ownership_parallel_plans.md`, parallel plans on a shared file are forbidden. Only Plan 02 (firestore.rules — disjoint file) can run in parallel with Plan 01. Waves 2-4 serialize the remaining proposal-modal.js plans. Adds wall-clock time but eliminates merge-conflict risk.

decisions.decision: Skipped UI-SPEC.md and RESEARCH.md for this phase
decisions.rationale: CONTEXT.md D-25 already specified the visual treatment (padding ~24px, font 12px, dot color gray #94a3b8, pill reuse, oldest-first sort, connector stub). CONTEXT also cited every line number for the code surfaces. Generating UI-SPEC.md and RESEARCH.md would duplicate work captured in CONTEXT. User confirmed via AskUserQuestion.

decisions.decision: Accepted iter-2 plan-checker INFOs without further revision
decisions.rationale: Iter-2 checker raised 1 WARNING (line 160 sort comparator — addressed in iter-2 revision) + 1 cosmetic INFO (off-by-one line numbers 528→529). Iter-3 checker not re-run because (a) the disk content was manually verified to match the revision spec, (b) running a third checker cycle on a tightly-bounded narrow change introduces noise risk, (c) we were already at the 3-iteration cap so no further revisions could be issued anyway.

next_action: /gsd-execute-phase 87.2 — starts Wave 1 (parallel: 87.2-01 proposal-modal.js + 87.2-02 firestore.rules). After Wave 1 commits successfully, pause and run `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules` before continuing to Wave 2 (which depends on the new rules being live for the operations_user smoke-test).
context_notes: Phase 87.2 was inserted 2026-05-21 after a super_admin walkthrough of PROP-2026-001 surfaced 5 integration gaps in the proposal workflow built across Phase 87 / 87.1. This is a POLISH phase — no new lifecycle stages, no schema migrations, no new collections. All work is targeted edits to existing files. Plans are commit-ready and execution-ready. The one cross-wave manual gate is the `firebase deploy` for the firestore.rules change in Plan 02 — without it, operations_user assignment-check writes will be denied at the rules layer and the Wave 2 smoke-test will fail in a confusing way (looks like a UI bug when actually it's a rules deploy missing). Branch: v3.3.
